3fXpb2bDlJ.insight: <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" />'s kit synergizes with <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Electrocute" /> for both trades and all-ins. Her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E" /> contributes twice towards it, meaning she just needs to proc her passive auto-attack or land another ability to guarantee it. \n\n* <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" /> tends to favor shorter trades until she gets her opponent into lethal range for an all-in, and <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Electrocute" /> is a major source of damage for both trades and kills.

qYyXvprkc4.insight: <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Sudden Impact" /> is mandatory for <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" />. She can proc it with her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E1" /> without needing to <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="E2" />, both instances of her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" />, and with her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" />. \n\n* With how easy it is to activate, this rune is just a free source of damage you don't need to think about when trading and going for all-ins.

lzZf9kAoIO.insight:
<ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Second Wind" /> / <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Bone Plating" /> compensates for <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" />'s lack of sustain in lane prior to buying <ScItemInline itemName="Hextech Gunblade" />

* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Bone Plating" /> versus champions that initiate extended trades and all-ins (eg. <ScChampionInline championId="Jax" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Garen" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Camille" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Gwen" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Kled" />) 
* Take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Second Wind" /> versus champions with shorter trades (eg. <ScChampionInline championId="Ornn" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Aurora" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Ambessa" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Jayce" />). If you're not sure about your matchup, take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Second Wind" /> as it is always useful whereas <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Bone Plating" /> requires mastery of trade timings.

IOnpL4D5HK.insight:
<ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Overgrowth" /> allows <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" /> to scale her durability into the mid-late game without investing gold into tank items. 

* However you should take  <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Unflinching" /> vs all-in champions such as <ScChampionInline championId="Darius" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Olaf" /> <ScChampionInline championId="Jax" /> where you need immediate combat value 
* Consider going <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Unflinching" /> in matchups where you might want to take <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Bone Plating" />.

fJjUEe4Mzr.insight: <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" /> ==does== ==not== build any Ability Haste, so  <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Ultimate Hunter" /> has insane cost efficient value because Ability Haste has higher value the **less** of it you have. \n\n* This lets <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" /> have ==more== ==fight== ==timers== with her <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="R" /> available which obviously allows her to have way more team fight impact than she otherwise would if it's on cool-down.

runesv3.summonerSpellExplanation: You can also take <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Teleport" /> + <ScSummonerInline summonerIdentifier="Ignite" /> in harder lanes for better recalls.

itemGroups.title: Core Build
itemGroups.insight: <ScItemInline itemName="Hextech Gunblade" /> buildpath is very efficient for <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" />, who has both AP and AD ratios, alongside <ScItemInline itemName="Hextech Alternator" /> increasing kill pressure. The active effect is a death sentence for squishies. Buy <ScItemInline itemName="Sorcerer's Shoes" /> second. Then buy  <ScItemInline itemName="Shadowflame" /> for more oneshot.

If the enemy team has 4 or more squishy champions, <ScItemInline itemName="Stormsurge" /> will allow you to blow them up even quicker. If the enemy team only has 2-3 squishies, build <ScItemInline itemName="Zhonya's Hourglass" /> to increase your survivability alongside <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" />
itemGroups.title: Fourth Item Options
Fifth Item Options
itemGroups.insight: If you did not build <ScItemInline itemName="Stormsurge" /> then skip it entirely. Build <ScItemInline itemName="Rabadon's Deathcap" /> if survivability is not an issue, otherwise build <ScItemInline itemName="Zhonya's Hourglass" />. <ScItemInline itemName="Void Staff" /> is only required if the enemy team is stacking multiple MR items.

Round out <ScChampionInline championId="Akali" />'s build with more damage, build whichever you're still missing between <ScItemInline itemName="Void Staff" /> and <ScItemInline itemName="Rabadon's Deathcap" />. Ultimately, you want both of these items in your inventory.
itemGroups.title: Sixth Item Options

startingItems.sectionExplainer: Start <ScItemInline itemName="Doran's Ring" /> in most matchups, unless versus Ranged champions - then you should start <ScItemInline itemName="Doran's Shield" /> instead.

insights.reason:
<ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" /> at level 2 gives you a crucial defensive option. The shroud restores energy on cast and lets you disengage from bad trades by going invisible.

* This ability procs <ScRuneInline runeIdentifier="Sudden Impact" />, instantly making your trading pattern much stronger than at level 1.
* Drop <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" /> after a short trade to deny the enemy's return damage and reset the fight on your terms
* The energy restore means you can ==play== ==more== ==aggressively== ==with== <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> ==at== ==level== ==2,== knowing <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="W" /> will let you escape if they try to extend the trade. Also, you can use the energy restore to help you farm under tower.
insights.title: Deny Return Trades
Lvl 4 Spike
Lvl 9 Spike
insights.reason:
At level 9, Akali's <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> reaches max rank with its ==lowest== ==energy== ==cost,== allowing you to have far more spell rotations available during extended fights and skirmishes.

* Max rank <ScAbilityInline_v2 spellKey="Q" /> is your biggest mid-game power spike  you can now spam abilities in teamfights without running dry on energy
* This is your **strongest** **1v1** **window** before enemies build defensive items  look for aggressive plays and side lane pressure. If you have <ScItemInline itemName="Hextech Gunblade" />, you win basically every 1v1, or can outsustain your opponents and win with an HP advantage later.
